categories.spatial_orientation: Chunk-index scrolling and contiguous context retrieval
categories.structural_aggregation: Heading hierarchy reconstruction and section-level filtering
categories.lexical_vs_semantic: Dense vs sparse vector signal differentiation
categories.corpus_lineage: Cross-document metadata traversal and timeline building
categories.analytical_deep_dives: Metadata auditing, token density, and context window management

prompts.prompt: Can you show me the paragraph that comes right after this one in a document?
If a text passage seems cut off, how do I get the very next chunk from the same document?
How do I retrieve the first two chunks from the beginning of a paper by index?
Given a document chunk, how can I find out how long the full document is and read its final paragraph?
How do I scroll sequentially through chunks until I reach the end of a specific argument, then summarize the entire contiguous block?
Given a chunk containing a variable reference, how do I navigate backward through preceding chunks to find its initial mathematical definition?
How do I retrieve adjacent chunks in both directions to provide a full context window around a specific methodological claim?
How do I step through sequential chunks from a starting point until the topic shifts entirely away from a given subject like embodied agents?
How do I iteratively expand the context window by fetching adjacent chunks until the underlying methodology transitions into empirical results?
Given a source file, how do I audit the chunk sequence from index 10 to 20 to verify no critical parsing steps were omitted?
How can I find the main heading of the section that a given text passage belongs to?
If I have a text chunk, is there a way to tell whether it is from the introduction or the conclusion of a document?
How do I retrieve all text under a specific heading level 1, such as a section titled ALFWORLD?
How can I isolate just the methodology section of a paper by filtering for its section title?
Given a paragraph chunk, how do I traverse the document structure upward to map its parent and grandparent headings?
How do I gather all chunks under a specific sub-heading and synthesize them into a single bulleted summary?
How do I scan a document's metadata to extract all level 2 headings and generate a table of contents?
How do I compare the content of chunks under the abstract heading with chunks under the conclusion heading to check for consistency?
How do I aggregate all chunks sharing a parent heading level across a document and evaluate their collective alignment with the thesis statement?
Given a text block, how do I map its hierarchical relationship by extracting its heading lineage and cross-referencing it with the document's global structure?
Is there a way to check if a document mentions a specific term like MinerU anywhere else in the text?
How can I find other documents that talk about the same general concept even if they use completely different words?
How do I search for the exact acronym RRF instead of getting documents generally about search algorithms?
How do I find chunks that discuss semantic alignment conceptually while requiring them to contain the exact keyword cosine?
How do I compare the results from querying semantic chunking using exact keyword matching versus general conceptual similarity?
How do I search for text related to memory architectures while filtering out any results that do not contain the specific word agentic?
How do I execute a hybrid search for document parsing while prioritizing the sparse vector signal to ensure exact layout terminology matches?
How do I retrieve chunks conceptually similar to a reference passage while filtering out any that share the same exact sparse keywords to guarantee diverse phrasing?
How do I execute a dual-signal retrieval targeting the conceptual framework of multi-agent systems while anchoring the sparse vector strictly to the embodied-agents taxonomy?
How do I evaluate the discrepancy between dense and sparse retrieval ranks for a query to determine whether the terminology has semantic overlap?
Are there any other papers in the same category that talk about embodied environments?
How do I filter my search results to only show chunks from a specific source file like 2010_03768.pdf?
How do I cross-reference findings in a chunk with other documents sharing the exact same subcategory?
How do I pull abstract chunks from all source files published in the year 2020 that discuss text alignment?
How do I find other chunks written by a specific author like Mohit Shridhar and compare their methodologies to one presented here?
How do I use an arxiv ID to fetch all connected documents and trace how a specific mathematical proof evolved across papers?
How do I aggregate chunks from a category of papers published before 2022 and contrast them with papers published after 2024 on the same topic?
How do I construct a timeline of advancements in a research area by sequentially pulling conclusion chunks sorted by their publish date?
How do I systematically trace the evolution of a specific algorithm by retrieving chunks across chronologically ordered source files matching a given overarching category?
How do I perform a multi-hop traversal linking the authors of a chunk to their prior works across different categories to identify methodological throughlines?
How can I check how many tokens are in a chunk and whether it is large enough to represent a complete thought?
If a chunk is only 32 tokens long, how do I automatically fetch the rest of the page to get more context?
How do I check whether a chunk's metadata indicates it has heading context, and if not, retrieve the nearest heading to verify the topic?
How do I analyze a source file's total chunk count to decide whether to summarize it section by section or all at once?
Before answering a query, how do I verify if a retrieved chunk is an isolated table or equation by checking its token density and adjacent context?
When retrieval results seem contradictory, how do I use the publish date metadata to determine if one chunk supersedes another?
How do I audit retrieved context for factual contradictions by verifying source file lineage and dynamically querying for missing intermediary chunks?
How do I optimize a context window by dropping chunks with redundant heading levels and replacing them with high-density methodology chunks?
How do I automatically trigger a small-to-big retrieval pattern when an initially fetched chunk falls below a 50-token threshold to ensure robust grounding?
How do I establish a continuous evaluation loop that checks the doc type of incoming chunks to dynamically alter the generation prompt's tone and constraints?
